job.title: Senior Applications and Development Manager
job.location: Arsenal Football Club, London, N5 1BU, GB
job.html_description:
The Role

We are looking for a hands-on Senior Applications and Development Manager to drive the world‑class development of our business‑critical applications and software solutions. Leading a team of Developers, Analysts and application specialists, you will oversee the full lifecycle of all applications and software, ensuring security requirements, onboarding processes and technology standards are consistently met.

In this exciting role, you will manage development pipelines, oversee application lifecycles, and collaborate with a wide range of stakeholders to deliver innovation and efficiency across our Technology department. You will be entrusted to lead a team responsible for ensuring applications remain secure, scalable and fully aligned with evolving business needs.

This is an excellent opportunity to shape the maintenance, optimisation and ongoing improvement of technology across our club - combining effective leadership with exceptional application development to deliver world‑class software solutions.

Who we are

We are one of the most famous clubs in world football, with a rich heritage and history of success – read more about our history, here.

Beyond that, we are passionate about our local community and, behind the scenes, we have a wide variety of opportunities and career paths for all. We have a very defined purpose: to act for a winning team, culture and community. We achieve this by ensuring we are courageous in the pursuit of progress, we champion our community and each other, and that we do the right thing (even when no one is looking).

Our Commitment to Safeguarding

We are committed to ensuring that our environments for children, young people and adults at risk remain safe and inclusive. We therefore require successful candidates to complete a DBS check and other essential background checks prior to starting their employment. Depending on the responsibilities of the role, a DBS will be either a basic, standard, or enhanced check.

Your day-to-day

  • Lead and empower your team - you’ll guide Developers, Analysts, and application specialists, supporting growth while consistently ensuring high‑quality delivery

  • Set development standards - you will outline and maintain best practices and governance frameworks that support secure and efficient application development

  • Maintain application performance - you will ensure systems remain secure, scalable, and up to date, managing upgrades and lifecycle activities

  • Collaborate with stakeholders - you will work closely with cross-functional teams to align on requirements, communicate progress, and deliver solutions that meet expectations

  • Manage external partners - you’ll oversee relationships with third‑party vendors, ensuring effective management of delivery and budget

  • Ensure compliance and mitigate risk - you will uphold regulatory, security, and governance requirements across all application development activity

 

What we are looking for

  • Application development experience - you have proven experience working in application development, ideally in football or sports environments

  • Demonstrated leadership expertise - you’re highly skilled in managing Software Development teams, supporting direct reports and leading team projects

  • Operational resilience and risk awareness - you can expertly analyse issues, assess risks, manage incidents and design effective security solutions

  • Exceptional knowledge of programming languages - you’re confident in understanding and interpreting programming languages (eg. Java, .NET & Python)

  • Cloud platform experience - you’re familiar with cloud platforms (Azure, AWS) and DevOps practices

  • Excellent communication skills - you possess exceptional written and verbal communication capabilities, with the ability to make technical concepts accessible to non-technical audiences

  • Software development methodology proficiency - you’re experienced in methodologies and frameworks such as SDLC, Agile and DevOps

  • Organised, strategic and adaptable mindset - you excel in navigating ambiguity and fast-changing environments, delivering smart, compelling solutions while managing projects simultaneously

Why choose us

At Arsenal, we want everyone to feel a sense of trust and belonging, so we are proud of both our club values and also what we offer to our employees. As one of our Gunners, you will receive:

  • An exciting reward and recognition scheme

  • Generous holiday allowance which increases with your length of service

  • Great internal learning and development programmes

  • A flexible hybrid working model

  • Priority access to apply for match tickets

  • A competitive health and wellbeing benefits package

  • A leading Employee Assistance Programme

  • Great discounts with some of our Partners

  • An opportunity to play a vital role in delivering our ED&I strategy by joining one of our Employee Networks

Discover why ‘Our Gunners’ are proud to be part of our team - view their stories here.

Arsenal for Everyone

Arsenal for Everyone is our commitment to promoting and embracing equality, diversity and inclusion, so that everyone connected to the club feels like they belong to the same Arsenal family. We believe that diversity of background, skills and experience drives our success on and off the pitch. 


Disability Confident Leader

We are a Disability Confident Leader. We actively welcome and encourage applications from people with disabilities and long-term health conditions. If you need disability-related adjustments to the recruitment process, please indicate this in your application.

If you are likely to meet the definition of being a ‘disabled person’ according to the Equality Act 2010, you may be eligible to apply for an interview via the Disability Confident Scheme - please indicate this in your application form below. The information you share with us about your health or disability will not be used in recruitment decisions.


Application Closing Date - Friday 6th March 2026

Please note: we reserve the right to close the position early if application volumes are particularly high. We encourage you to get your application in sooner rather than later.

Updates on the status of your application will be sent via email, please routinely check your spam and junk folders, as occasionally emails sent via the Teamtailor platform will find themselves there.

Good luck!

job.title: People Services Administrator
job.location: Arsenal Football Club, London, N5 1BU, GB
job.html_description:
The Role

We are looking for a People Services Administrator to provide high impact, value add People/HR support services to our managers, colleagues and the wider People team.

This exciting opportunity will allow you to play a pivotal role in supporting employees at all levels; you will be the first point of contact for our people and through practical employee lifecycle administration, you will ensure everyone gets a consistently brilliant experience with the People team.

 

Who we are

We are one of the most famous clubs in world football, with a rich heritage and history of success – read more about our history, here.

Beyond that, we are passionate about our local community and, behind the scenes, we have a wide variety of opportunities and career paths for all. We have a very defined purpose: to act for a winning team, culture and community. We achieve this by ensuring we are courageous in the pursuit of progress, we champion our community and each other, and that we do the right thing (even when no one is looking).

Our Commitment to Safeguarding

We are committed to ensuring that our environments for children, young people and adults at risk remain safe and inclusive. We therefore require successful candidates to complete a DBS check and other essential background checks prior to starting their employment. Depending on the responsibilities of the role, a DBS will be either a basic, standard, or enhanced check.

Your day-to-day

  • Handle first-line query management - you’ll deliver prompt, accurate and supportive responses to a range of queries, providing clear guidance aligned with policies, processes and people practices

  • Provide comprehensive data and systems administration - you’ll ensure accuracy when updating HR systems and electronic files for new starters, changes, absences and leavers

  • Champion efficient document creation - you’ll prepare a high volume of varied contracts and agreements, working with pace and accuracy, ensuring all details are fully checked and compliant before issuing

  • Oversee onboarding and compliance coordination - you’ll oversee pre‑employment checks, including DBS and right‑to‑work, with thorough attention to accuracy, timelines and regulatory requirements

  • Support the payroll and employee lifecycle - you’ll prepare payroll inputs and reports to a high standard and contribute to annual people routines such as pay review, bonus cycles, performance cycles and surveys

  • Aid financial processing - you’ll raise purchase orders and approve invoices promptly and accurately, ensuring alignment with internal financial procedures and documentation requirements

  • Partner with our wider People team - you’ll provide reliable support across operations, talent development, reward and talent acquisition when required

  • Uphold compliance and governance - you’ll apply GDPR, employment legislation and internal policies effectively, producing well‑structured notes for employee relations casework and demonstrating fairness and inclusion in all interactions

 

What we are looking for

  • Track record in a People or HR team - you bring hands‑on experience having managed day‑to‑day HR and People administration, maintaining accurate records, documentation and databases with precision

  • Trusted first point of contact - you’ve supported a wider HR or People team, taking pride in delivering timely, end‑to‑end resolutions to employee queries

  • On boarding and off boarding expert - you have proactively led processes across multiple systems to prepare contracts and complete a range of vetting checks with confidence and efficiency

  • Proactive payroll support experience - you have provided accurate and timely employee data, including starters, leavers, absences and contractual changes, taking full ownership of data integrity

  • Employee focused mindset - you're genuinely passionate about helping others and providing a reliable and approachable service

  • Confident communicator - you communicate clearly and professionally across written, verbal and in‑person interactions, building impactful and effective working relationships

  • Proactive and accountable approach - you take ownership of your work, ensuring every task is driven through to completion and issues are resolved but also understand when to escalate and reach out for support

  • Exceptional attention to detail - you take pride in producing accurate work and spotting potential inconsistencies early

We encourage you to submit a cover letter outlining how you meet our criteria.

Why choose us

At Arsenal, we want everyone to feel a sense of trust and belonging, so we are proud of both our club values and also what we offer to our employees. As one of our Gunners, you will receive:

  • An exciting reward and recognition scheme

  • Generous holiday allowance which increases with your length of service

  • Great internal learning and development programmes

  • A flexible hybrid working model

  • Priority access to apply for match tickets

  • A competitive health and wellbeing benefits package

  • A leading Employee Assistance Programme

  • Great discounts with some of our Partners

  • An opportunity to play a vital role in delivering our ED&I strategy by joining one of our Employee Networks

Discover why ‘Our Gunners’ are proud to be part of our team - view their stories here.

Arsenal for Everyone

Arsenal for Everyone is our commitment to promoting and embracing equality, diversity and inclusion, so that everyone connected to the club feels like they belong to the same Arsenal family. We believe that diversity of background, skills and experience drives our success on and off the pitch. 


Disability Confident Leader

We are a Disability Confident Leader. We actively welcome and encourage applications from people with disabilities and long-term health conditions. If you need disability-related adjustments to the recruitment process, please indicate this in your application.

If you are likely to meet the definition of being a ‘disabled person’ according to the Equality Act 2010, you may be eligible to apply for an interview via the Disability Confident Scheme - please indicate this in your application form below. The information you share with us about your health or disability will not be used in recruitment decisions.


Application Closing Date - Sunday 8th March 2026

Please note: we reserve the right to close the position early if application volumes are particularly high. We encourage you to get your application in sooner rather than later.

Updates on the status of your application will be sent via email, please routinely check your spam and junk folders, as occasionally emails sent via the Teamtailor platform will find themselves there.

Good luck!

Application Closing Date - Sunday 8th March 2026

Updates on the status of your application will be sent via email, please routinely check your spam and junk folders, as occasionally emails sent via the Teamtailor platform will find themselves there.

job.title: Careers
job.html_description:
Careers

Working at Chelsea

As a team we’re hardworking, resilient, brave and proud. We constantly strive to do the right thing. We’re involved and highly committed, taking challenges in our stride and focusing on improving and evolving. We work to win and are driven to create optimum business performance. Our success is underpinned by our diverse and inclusive attitude, creating an environment where everyone is welcome and treated equally. If you’re motivated and want to help us win more trophies, you’ve got a place here at Chelsea.

Current Opportunities

Our Values

We have more than 150 unique roles that help us reach our goals. Whether it’s by supporting the teams on the pitch in our coaching, medical, sport science, analytics, player recruitment or grounds teams or off the pitch, in departments such as venue and stadium operations, sponsorship, marketing, legal, finance and HR. No matter what our teams do, they’re brave and decisive and always look for new ways to stand out from the crowd. Our Values are important to us.

Our Values

London Living Wage

We are proud to be a London Living Wage Employer. Our continued commitment as a Living Wage employer means we raised our minimum rate of pay in 2020. We were also the first English professional football club to be accredited by the Living Wage Foundation.

Safeguarding

Chelsea Football Club are committed to creating a safe environment in which the welfare of children, young people and adults at risk are front and centre at the club. Safeguarding is of paramount importance and we expect all colleagues and persons associated with working at Chelsea to share this commitment. We are committed to doing the right thing. Therefore, we require candidates to undergo child protection screening appropriate to the post, including checks with past employers and the Disclosure and Barring Service.

Our commitment to Equality, Diversity and Inclusion

At Chelsea we recognise that the diversity of our people is one of our greatest strengths and we are taking positive action to ensure our existing colleagues and job applicants can fully be themselves and bring their own unique experiences and perspectives to Chelsea FC. This means giving full and fair consideration to all applicants regardless of age, disability, gender reassignment, race, religion or belief, sex, sexual orientation, marriage and civil partnership, and pregnancy and maternity. Alongside our No To Hate campaign, Chelsea FC takes all allegations of discrimination extremely seriously and encourages a culture where individuals feel they can seek out support if they ever witness or experience discrimination. In line with our responsibilities under the Equality Act 2010, Chelsea FC takes all allegations of discrimination extremely seriously and encourages a culture where individuals feel they can seek out support if they ever witness or experience discrimination.

Inclusive Employers - experts in diversity and inclusion

Get in Contact

Additional Enquiries

Our committed team is readily available to provide guidance and assistance. Feel free to opt for either calling or emailing us, and our team will reach out to you promptly. (International: +44 207 386 9373)

International: +44 207 386 9373

job.title: Employee belonging to a protected category
job.location: Viale della Liberazione, Milano, MI, 20124, IT
job.html_description:
Are you a dynamic, precise person interested in the world of sport, media & entertainment? Would you like to join an international club and work in a young and stimulating environment? FC Internazionale is constantly looking for professionals belonging to the Protected Categories (art 1 l.68/99) with whom to get in touch in order to evaluate together future collaboration possibilities for one of our departments, such as, for example:


  • Supply Chain and Procurement
  • Technology
  • E-commerce and Product Development
  • Marketing and CRM
  • Social Media & Digital
  • Ticketing & Contact Centre

In order to best align your interests and our needs, please specify function and area of potential interest when sending your application. Would you like to join our team? Then what are you waiting for, send us your CV! We look forward to getting to know you!


Those most in line may be evaluated for future needs to strengthen company structures.

job.title: Head Of Performance - Men's 1st Team
job.html_description:
At Juventus, performance It’s a culture.
It’s how we prepare, protect, and push our athletes to compete at the highest level, every single day.

We’re looking for a Head of Performance to lead the strategic integration and continuous optimisation of our multidisciplinary performance function, working at the heart of an elite football environment.

🎯 The challenge
You will act as the central performance integrator, aligning sports science, strength & conditioning, nutrition, and return‑to‑play to maximise player availability, physical output, and long‑term athlete development.
From first team to academy pathway, you’ll translate scientific insight into applied football practice — driving evidence‑based decisions that make a real impact on the pitch.

⚽ What you’ll lead

  • A world‑class performance programme for the Men’s First Team

  • Integrated frameworks for load management, injury risk reduction, and return‑to‑play

  • Cross‑functional collaboration with technical and medical leadership

  • Performance intelligence that supports strategic and match‑critical decision‑making

👤 Who we’re looking for

You'll bring a rare mix of scientific expertise, applied football experience, and leadership, including:

  • At least 10 years of experience in Performance roles within professional football

  • Proven delivery of elite football performance and periodisation models, including RTP and athletic development

  • Advanced expertise in performance technologies (GPS, AMS, testing systems, gym programming)

  • Strong capability to monitor, interpret, and prescribe load using multi‑source physiological data

  • Deep knowledge of injury epidemiology, reconditioning principles, and RTP progressions in elite football

  • Confidence in communicating complex performance data clearly to players, coaches, and staff

  • Experience in integrating multidisciplinary teams, breaking silos and driving shared performance objectives

  • Sound judgement in high‑pressure, time‑critical decisions (match‑day availability, congested schedules)

  • A track record of developing staff and scaling performance structures within elite environments

🚀 Why Juventus

Because here, performance means competing in another league — every day.
You’ll work in a club where ambition, innovation, and accountability set the standard, and where your expertise will directly shape how we prepare to win.

👉 Ready to make performance count?
Apply now and help define the future of elite football performance at Juventus.

👉 Ready to make performance count? Apply now and help define the future of elite football performance at Juventus.

job.title: Kitchen Team Leader
job.html_description:
Join our team at the AXA Training Centre and play a vital role in keeping our kitchens running smoothly and efficiently. We’re looking for someone with strong kitchen porter experience. You’ll ensure all catering goods are ordered, received, and distributed accurately and on time — while also supporting with travel packing and loading when needed.

Key Responsibilities

  • Support the Head Chef and Front of House Manager in ensuring smooth back-of-house operations.
  • Manage food, chemical, and cleaning stock levels using Procure Wizard, ensuring accurate ordering, storage, and rotation.
  • Oversee food deliveries, storage, and stock control, maintaining full compliance with food safety standards.
  • Lead, rota, and support the kitchen porter team to maintain high hygiene and cleanliness standards across all kitchen areas.
  • Monitor food safety using Navitas systems, ensuring correct temperatures and traceability for all products.
  • Ensure all food hygiene, COSHH, and health & safety policies are consistently met and documented.
  • Process purchase orders, delivery notes, and invoices accurately and on time.
  • Conduct regular stock checks and work with external auditors to ensure accurate stock counts.
  • Assist the Head Chef with supplier management, ensuring approved suppliers meet quality and safety standards.
  • Complete all required paperwork, reports, and compliance checks in line with company policies.
  • Conduct annual performance reviews for direct reports and support team development.

 Essential Requirements

  • Strong experience as a lead kitchen porter/ kitchen assistant, or similar role.
  • Proven stock management skills with attention to accuracy and organisation.
  • Good knowledge of food safety and HACCP procedures.
  • Confident using Microsoft Office and other computer systems.
  • Strong communication skills with the ability to work effectively with chefs, managers, and colleagues.
  • Positive, professional, and team-focused approach to work.

Why should you apply?

This is a full-time permanent role working 35 hours per week, 5 from 7 days, including evenings and weekends. Your main base will be our AXA Training Centre. 

To reward your hard work and commitment we offer a competitive salary, 25 days holiday (plus 8 bank holidays and the option to purchase up to an additional 5 days) and a contributory pension scheme.

You will have access to our benefits kit bag where you can get high street discounts, and a selection of benefit schemes you can join. There are opportunities to get involved with volunteering through our LFC Foundation to give back to the local community.

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code, and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

job.title: Warehouse Assistant, DC Operations Support
job.html_description:
We have an exciting opportunity for an individual to join our Liverpool FC team as a Warehouse Assistant, Troubleshooter located at our distribution centre. 

What will you be doing?

In this role, you will act as a key technical and operational resource across all warehouse functions. You’ll support Supervisors and colleagues by providing hands‑on operational expertise, resolving issues on the floor, and helping maintain smooth operational workflow across the Distribution Centre.

Operational Support

  • Competently perform all warehouse tasks (inbound, picking, packing, replenishment, outbound, returns, and production/personalisation) with efficiency and accuracy.
  • Act as the first point of support when operational issues arise, resolving problems quickly and escalating where appropriate.
  • Provide cover during peak periods, colleague absences, or unexpected operational challenges.

Stock Control & Accuracy

  • Support all stock management activities including scanning, put‑away, picking, cycle counts, and reconciliation.
  • Investigate discrepancies and errors in stock movements, providing solutions or escalating recurring patterns to Supervisors.
  • Assist in periodic inventory counts (PI) and ensure accurate stock records and reporting.
  • Model and support loss‑prevention practices and proper stock‑handling procedures.

Training & Coaching Support

  • Mentor and coach new or less‑experienced warehouse operatives on operational procedures and best practices.
  • Support Supervisors in delivering training on SOPs, systems, equipment use, and safety processes.
  • Share expertise across shifts to help raise overall team capability and confidence.

Support for Supervisors

  • Provide additional operational oversight and support to Supervisors during the shift.
  • Monitor team compliance with SOPs, safety requirements, and quality standards, raising concerns when necessary.
  • Recommend process improvements based on hands‑on operational experience.

Health, Safety & Compliance

  • Ensure full compliance with safety policies, PPE requirements, and safe working practices.
  • Conduct equipment checks and report any basic maintenance needs (FLT, scanners, conveyors, packing equipment).
  • Support investigations into incidents, near‑misses, or safety breaches as required.

Problem Solving & Continuous Improvement

  • Identify inefficiencies, bottlenecks, and recurring issues in real‑time and recommend improvements.
  • Support Supervisors and Operations Managers with process improvement initiatives.
  • Champion change on the warehouse floor, promoting adoption of new systems, tools or ways of working.

Operational Administration Support

  • Assist with day‑to‑day administrative tasks relating to stock, production documentation, returns, or reporting.
  • Ensure data is accurately captured in WMS/ERP systems in line with site standards.
  • Support ad‑hoc reporting or investigation requests from Supervisors or Operations Managers.

Who are we looking for? 

  • Experience working within a distribution centre
  • Leadership skills and the confidence to support colleagues
  • Strong teamwork ethic and communication skills
  • Retail operations knowledge
  • Excellent attention to detail
  • Sound IT knowledge
  • Flexible and adaptive approach to work
  • WMS experience
  • Experience working in an automated or system‑driven environment

Why should you apply?

This is a full‑time permanent position working 35 hours per week, across any five days out of seven, which will include regular weekend working. Your main base will be our Distribution Centre, in Prescot.  

Please note, this vacancy may close early if we receive a high volume of applications, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

To reward your hard work and commitment we offer a competitive salary, 25 days holiday (plus 8 bank holidays and the option to purchase up to an additional 5 days) and access to our benefits kit bag which includes but not limited to:

  • Contributory pension scheme matching up to 5.5%
  • Life Assurance Cover
  • Free financial guidance and mortgage advice
  • Car salary sacrifice scheme for affordable driving
  • Cycle to Work scheme to keep you active
  • Purchase season ticket loans for easier commuting
  • Exclusive shopping discounts & cashback with top retailers
  • Will Writing Service for future planning
  • Employee Assistance Programme for confidential support
  • Medicash Health Cash Plan for everyday healthcare needs
  • Volunteering opportunities to give back to the community
  • Special LFC perks – retail discounts, partner offers, free LFCTV GO access, and priority tickets for matches, events & concerts

…and much more! LFC Benefits.pdf

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment. 

#LFCJobs

job.title: Casual Kitchen Assistants
job.html_description:
We are looking for experienced Kitchen Assistants to join our casual catering team at Anfield Stadium. You’ll work in our state-of-the-art kitchens alongside our talented and creative chefs, supporting the delivery of first-class food and service on match days and events.

 What will you be doing?

  • Assisting with basic food preparation.
  • Unloading and storing deliveries safely and efficiently.
  • Maintaining cleanliness by emptying bins, sweeping, and mopping floors.
  • Ensuring all kitchen equipment is cleaned and stored correctly.
  • Deep cleaning fridges, storage areas, and other kitchen spaces to maintain hygiene standards.
  • Operating automated dishwashers to clean crockery and utensils used by guests.
  • Supporting with porter duties, ensuring all back-of-house areas are tidy and ready for the next event.

Who are we looking for?

We’re looking for hardworking individuals who thrive in a fast-paced kitchen environment.

Please note you must be 18 or over to be considered for this position.

 You’ll have:

  • Previous experience as a Kitchen Assistant, Kitchen Porter, or in a similar role.
  • A good understanding of manual handling techniques and safe working practices.
  • Experience using a range of kitchen equipment.
  • A positive attitude and a commitment to maintaining high standards of cleanliness and teamwork.

Why should you apply?

This is a casual position, offering variable hours including evenings and weekends on a flexible assignment basis.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

job.title: Casual Match Day Stewards
job.html_description:
Here at Liverpool Football Club, we are looking for Casual Match Day Stewards to join our team.

As part of the recruitment process, shortlisted candidates will be invited to attend a Group Induction & Interview. During the induction, members of our Safety Team will introduce the role, outline key responsibilities, and provide essential information. This will be followed by individual 121 interviews to assess your suitability for the position.

Please submit your application for the role to be considered for invitation to a Group Induction & Interview session.

What will you be doing in the role?

  • Working as part of a team to provide a safe and secure environment for match day events.
  • Ensuring compliance with stadium regulations, keeping spectators safe and informed.
  • Responding effectively to any incidents of disorder with a calm and focused approach.
  • Offering support and guidance to spectators when needed, ensuring they feel welcome and informed.
  • Conducting personal searches of spectators entering the stadium to maintain security.
  • Documenting and reporting incidents, ensuring clear and accurate records are kept.

We are looking for people who:

  • Have strong communication skills who can confidently engage with supporters, even in challenging situations. 
  • Can handle conflict calmly and professionally.
  • Can write clear reports and possess a basic understanding of conflict management.
  • Are over 18, due to the nature of the role.

If you are someone who can bring experience, calm, and a professional approach to this important role, we would love to hear from you.

By applying for the position of Casual Match Day Steward with Liverpool Football Club, please be aware that this role requires the completion of a Level 2 Certificate in Spectator Safety (as per the Sports Grounds Safety Authority), which must be completed within 12 months from the date of your first induction training.

By submitting your application, you confirm that you can commit to completing the Level 2 Certificate in Spectator Safety within 12 months from your first induction training. As this qualification is essential for the role, if you are unable to complete it within this timeframe, we may not be able to progress your application.

This is a casual position, offering variable hours including evenings and weekends on a flexible assignment basis.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

job.title: Employability Program Coordinator (US based)
job.html_description:
The LFC Foundation is the official charity of Liverpool Football Club. The Foundation has been delivering employability programs in Liverpool for many years and more recently in the US. This is an exciting opportunity to create and deliver high-quality employability programs to provide life-changing opportunities in the most deprived areas of New York and New Jersey, helping to raise the aspiration and job prospects for all participants.

THE LFC FOUNDATION’S WORKS PROGRAMS

This role will support the development of the LFC Foundation’s flagship international employability programs in New York and New Jersey. Working across all employability programs, including Creative Works, a creative-based skills development program and Soccer Works, a sport-based skills development program, which both develop the employability skills of young people at risk of long-term unemployment.

The Program Coordinator will be responsible for effective delivery of LFC Foundation employability program, taking participants from initial engagement during program delivery, through their mentoring and ultimately supporting their pathway into work experience and eventually meaningful paid employment.

Key Responsibilities

· Design and deliver engaging creative employability sessions that build skills and elevate career aspirations.

· Develop curriculum and collaborate with external partners to meet KPIs.

· Support participant identification and recruitment in coordination with UK-based teams.

· Coordinate program logistics including venues, transportation, meals and accommodations.

· Support the Senior Coordinator in building relationships with existing and new schools and organizations, and assisting with funder reporting.

· Facilitate high-quality work experience placements.

· Work closely with the Impact and Insights team to ensure the monitoring and evaluation framework is carried out effectively.

· Manage risk across employability programmes to ensure the safety and wellbeing of all young people and staff.

· Continually implement quality assurance procedures to ensure consistent delivery of a world-class programme.

· Embed the highest safeguarding standards across all areas of work.

Required Knowledge, Skills & Experience

· Experience planning and delivering creative educational workshops or training sessions.

· Experience working with vulnerable populations.

· Understanding of inclusive, youth-centered work practices.

· Knowledge of youth mentoring models.

· Experience guiding young adults to additional opportunities.

· Strong alignment with LFC Foundation mission and values.

· Excellent verbal, written, and presentation skills.

· Strong organizational and time-management skills.

· Experience within creative, community, or education settings.

· Knowledge of local industries in New York and/or New Jersey.

Technical Skills

· Safeguarding Young People – Level 1 (or U.S. equivalent).

· Proficiency in Microsoft Office 365 including Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.

· Experience collecting and managing program data

The role involves work across many locations throughout New York and New Jersey in order to deliver the programme in the community. So, if you enjoy making a difference to real people out in the community, this role is for you!

This is a Fixed Term Contract.

To reward your hard work and commitment we offer a competitive salary, 10 days holiday (plus national US holidays) and a contributory pension scheme. 

You will have access to our benefits kit bag where you can get high street discounts, and a selection of benefit schemes you can join. There are opportunities to get involved with volunteering through our LFC Foundation to give back to the local community.

Please note, this vacancy may close early if we receive a high volume of applications, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment. This role is subject to a satisfactory enhanced DBS check.

#LFCJobs

To reward your hard work and commitment we offer a competitive salary, 10 days holiday (plus national US holidays) and a contributory pension scheme.
You will have access to our benefits kit bag where you can get high street discounts, and a selection of benefit schemes you can join. There are opportunities to get involved with volunteering through our LFC Foundation to give back to the local community.
Please note, this vacancy may close early if we receive a high volume of applications, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.
At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.
Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment. This role is subject to a satisfactory enhanced DBS check.
#LFCJobs

job.title: Senior Manager, Partnerships Delivery
job.html_description:
We are looking for a Partnership Delivery - Senior Manager to join the team at Liverpool FC. 

Do you want to play a pivotal role in the Partnership Delivery Team at Liverpool FC, delivering best in class service levels, commercial relationships and activations, whilst driving our reputation and engagement with fans globally? 

We are looking for an experienced Senior Manager to join the Partnership Delivery Team, and you will be responsible for implementing flexible, partner centric servicing and ensuring strong relationship development and renewal of the key partnerships within your portfolio. You will be supported by a team, and you will lead in objective setting, strategic direction, creative development, and delivery of campaign effectiveness. You will be accountable for ensuring key deliverables are achieved to a high standard and that your team prioritises and communicates transparently and effectively.

What will you be doing?

  • Manage a diverse partnership portfolio, including one Principal Partner and multiple secondary partners.
  • Deliver full 360° account management across planning, activation, measurement, reporting, and feedback.
  • Work with Directors and peers to develop strategic plans that enhance partner servicing and maximise contractual rights.
  • Ensure transparency across the Partnerships Team to align partner objectives and maintain a coordinated activation calendar.
  • Build and maintain strong relationships with allocated partners, leading a structured contact strategy including regular calls, video meetings, and face‑to‑face sessions.
  • Maintain consistent, high‑quality communication with partner contacts and develop excellent working relationships.
  • Implement creative, insight‑led marketing activation plans that drive measurable ROI.
  • Lead partner reporting, providing data‑driven updates on global reach, KPI delivery, and ensuring accurate Cost of Sale documentation.
  • Maintain clear visibility of all partner activations, working closely with the Partnership Experience Team to align delivery.
  • Oversee the onboarding and activation of new partnership deals, collaborating with Directors, Commercial teams, and wider stakeholders.
  • Evolve team ways of working and ensure delivery against key objectives and deadlines.
  • Provide day‑to‑day line management, offering guidance to ensure best‑in‑class service from direct reports.
  • Manage team priorities, resources, travel, training, and Cost of Sale budgets across partner accounts.
  • Ensure regular information flow to and from the Directors of Partnership Delivery through structured meetings and status updates.
  • Maintain up‑to‑date knowledge of partner industries and market trends, working closely with the Insights Team.
  • Support leadership in benchmarking Liverpool FC’s partnership management against global sports and entertainment best practice.
  • Build strong, influential relationships with internal departments across the Club.
  • Participate in Club working groups and steering committees where required.
  • Attend matches, international events, industry gatherings, and partner meetings as appropriate.

Who are we looking for?

The successful candidate will bring a minimum of five years’ relevant experience within the sports or entertainment marketing industry, gained on the sponsor, agency, or rightsholder side. They will demonstrate strong integrated marketing knowledge and proven account management expertise across multiple clients or partners. A solid understanding of digital marketing and industry trends, combined with a methodical working style and a high level of accuracy and quality, is essential.

Why should you apply?

This is a full-time perm position working 35 hours per week. Your main base will be our city centre office in Liverpool.

Please note, this vacancy may close early if we receive a high volume of applications, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

To reward your hard work and commitment we offer a competitive salary, 25 days holiday (plus 8 bank holidays and the option to purchase up to an additional 5 days) and access to our benefits kit bag which includes but not limited to:

  • Contributory pension scheme matching up to 5.5%
  • Life Assurance Cover
  • Free financial guidance and mortgage advice
  • Car salary sacrifice scheme for affordable driving
  • Cycle to Work scheme to keep you active
  • Purchase season ticket loans for easier commuting
  • Exclusive shopping discounts & cashback with top retailers
  • Will Writing Service for future planning
  • Employee Assistance Programme for confidential support
  • Medicash Health Cash Plan for everyday healthcare needs
  • Volunteering opportunities to give back to the community
  • Special LFC perks – retail discounts, partner offers, free LFCTV GO access, and priority tickets for matches, events & concerts

…and much more! LFC Benefits.pdf

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

#LFCJobs

job.title: Casual Hospitality Hosts
job.html_description:
Liverpool Football Club is seeking confident, professional individuals who are passionate about providing exceptional customer service. As a Casual Hospitality Host, you will play a key role in creating a memorable experience for our guests during match days and events throughout the season.

This role offers the chance to work at one of the world’s most iconic football stadiums, in a supportive and professional team. With casual shifts and complimentary Stagecoach transport on match days. If you have a wealth of experience and a professional, friendly approach, this is a great opportunity to bring your skills to a dynamic environment.

What will you be doing in the role?

  • Conducting ticket checks, bag searches, and security wanding to ensure a safe environment for all guests.
  • Escorting guests to their designated boxes, suites, and tables with professionalism and care.
  • Overseeing guest access at doors, escalators, and lifts to ensure smooth and easy transitions.
  • Collaborating with the management team, stewards, and other safety hosts to ensure efficient operations.
  • Offering a high level of customer service to enhance the guest experience.

We are looking for people who:

  • Have excellent communication skills, and a calm and steady approach to challenges.
  • Have a commitment to providing excellent customer service. 
  • Have previous experience in front-of-house or customer service role.
  • Take pride in offering a great service and creating a welcoming environment.
  • Comfortable remaining in one position for extended periods during events.

 A strict dress code applies (all uniform will be provided).

By applying for the position of Casual Hospitality Host with Liverpool Football Club, please be aware that this role requires the completion of a Level 2 Certificate in Spectator Safety (as per the Sports Grounds Safety Authority), which must be completed within 12 months from the date of your first induction training.

By submitting your application, you confirm that you can commit to completing the Level 2 Certificate in Spectator Safety within 12 months from your first induction training. As this qualification is essential for the role, if you are unable to complete it within this timeframe, we may not be able to progress your application.

This is a casual position, offering variable hours including evenings and weekends on a flexible assignment basis.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

job.title: Casual Commis Chef
job.html_description:
Here at Liverpool Football Club, we are looking for Commis Chefs to join our casual culinary team.

As a Casual Commis Chef, you will support the senior chefs in delivering high-quality food across our stadium on a match day. This is a fantastic opportunity for someone at the early stages of their chef career who is eager to learn, develop their skills, and gain experience within a fast-paced stadium environment.

What will you be doing?

  • Assisting in the preparation and cooking of food to the highest standards.
  • Supporting the kitchen team.
  • Ensuring all food is prepared in line with recipes, portion control, and presentation standards.
  • Maintaining high levels of cleanliness and organisation within your section.
  • Following all food safety, allergen, and hygiene procedures at all times.
  • Supporting stock rotation and minimising waste in line with company guidelines.
  • Working collaboratively with colleagues to ensure smooth and efficient service.

Who are we looking for?

  • Some previous experience in a professional kitchen environment (placement, apprenticeship, or similar desirable).
  • A basic food hygiene certificate.
  • Ability to work in a fast-paced environment and remain calm under pressure.
  • Strong team player with a positive, proactive attitude.
  • Willingness to learn from senior chefs and take on feedback.

Why should you apply?

This is a great opportunity to build your culinary career within a dynamic and exciting environment. You will gain exposure to large-scale hospitality environment, working alongside experienced chefs who are passionate about developing talent.

This is a casual position, offering variable hours including evenings and weekends on a flexible assignment basis.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

job.title: Casual Chef de Partie
job.html_description:
Here at Liverpool Football Club, we are looking for experienced Chef de Parties to join our casual culinary team on a match day.

As a Casual Chef de Partie, you will take ownership of a designated section within the kitchen, supporting the delivery of high-quality food across our stadium hospitality on a match day. This is an exciting opportunity for a skilled and motivated chef looking to progress within a fast-paced, high-volume environment.

What will you be doing?

  • Leading and managing your designated section to ensure food is prepared and presented to the highest standards.
  • Preparing, cooking and finishing dishes in line with recipes, specifications and service requirements.
  • Supporting junior chefs and commis chefs with guidance, training and day to day supervision.
  • Ensuring consistency in quality, portion control and presentation.
  • Maintaining full compliance with food safety, allergen and hygiene regulations.
  • Monitoring stock levels within your section and supporting stock rotation to minimise waste.
  • Assisting senior chefs with menu preparation, service planning and kitchen organisation.
  • Contributing to a positive, professional and high-performing kitchen culture.

Who are we looking for?

  • Proven experience working at Chef de Partie level or in a similar role within a professional kitchen.
  • Strong technical cooking skills and a solid understanding of kitchen operations.
  • Level 2 Food Safety certificate (minimum).
  • Ability to lead by example and support the development of junior team members.
  • Calm and organised approach under pressure.
  • A genuine passion for food, quality and continuous improvement.

Why should you apply?

This is an excellent opportunity to join a dynamic culinary team. You will play a key role in delivering exceptional food experiences to guests and supporters.

This is a casual position, offering variable hours including evenings and weekends on a flexible assignment basis.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

job.title: LFCW Kit and Equipment Manager
job.location: AXA Melwood Training Centre, United Kingdom
job.html_description:
We have an exciting opportunity for an individual to join our LFC Women’s team as a Kit and Equipment Manager. 

Liverpool FC Women is embarking on an exciting and transformative phase, with a clear ambition to compete consistently at the top of the Women’s game. Central to this vision is the development of a world class team on and off the pitch. 

As part of this journey, we are seeking a highly skilled Kit & Equipment Manager to support Liverpool FC Women’s first team players and staff. This role is instrumental in the day-to-day operations of the football club and we are looking for an extremely organised individual with a keen eye for detail.

Working closely with cross functional teams including coaching, performance, medical and football leadership – you will be a flexible team player with strong time management skills. 

What will you be doing?

  • Lead the LFC Women Kit Department to ensure there are effective processes and practices in place that can service the team to the best possible standard; liaising with the LFCW management, coaching and performance staff on all matters concerning kit and equipment. 
  • Develop strong working relationships with LFC external partners and internal staff from cross-club departments. 
  • Develop and maintain an electronic monitoring and logging system for kit and equipment that provides live data of stock levels and planned distribution.
  • Ensuring the effective logistical planning for all required kit and equipment, attending home/away games as required in liaison with the Management and Coaching Staff.
  • Organise and co-ordinate all kit and equipment for away travel and the loading of club transport ready for departure.
  • Ensure all kit is fully laundered (washed and dried) correctly and packed away within the required timelines.
  • Maintain the quality and cleanliness of all kit and equipment assigned to LFCW.
  • Mark and maintain all kit with the relevant initials/numbers, as directed by the Operations Manager.
  • Maintain accurate records of all kit logs and report discrepancies to the Operations Manager.

What we are looking for: 

The successful candidate will hold a clean driving licence and demonstrate meticulous attention to detail, consistently upholding high standards of behaviour and appearance. Previous experience in inventory management is essential, along with a background in working within a professional football club or sports-based environment, particularly with high-performance athletes. A clean driving licence and having the flexibility to travel nationally and internationally are essential.  Candidates must possess certification in Safeguarding and Protecting Children (or FA equivalent) prior to starting the role. While not essential, the ability to speak additional languages and prior experience in the women’s game are highly desirable.

Please note, this vacancy may close early if we receive a high volume of applications, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

To reward your hard work and commitment we offer a competitive salary, 25 days holiday (plus 8 bank holidays and the option to purchase up to an additional 5 days) and access to our benefits kit bag which includes but not limited to:

  • Contributory pension scheme matching up to 5.5%
  • Life Assurance Cover
  • Free financial guidance and mortgage advice
  • Car salary sacrifice scheme for affordable driving
  • Cycle to Work scheme to keep you active
  • Purchase season ticket loans for easier commuting
  • Exclusive shopping discounts & cashback with top retailers
  • Will Writing Service for future planning
  • Employee Assistance Programme for confidential support
  • Medicash Health Cash Plan for everyday healthcare needs
  • Volunteering opportunities to give back to the community
  • Special LFC perks – retail discounts, partner offers, free LFCTV GO access, and priority tickets for matches, events & concerts

…and much more! LFC Benefits.pdf

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment. This role is subject to a satisfactory enhanced DBS check.

Working closely with cross functional teams including coaching, performance, medical and football leadership – you will be a flexible team player with strong time management skills.
What will you be doing?
- Lead the LFC Women Kit Department to ensure there are effective processes and practices in place that can service the team to the best possible standard; liaising with the LFCW management, coaching and performance staff on all matters concerning kit and equipment. - Develop strong working relationships with LFC external partners and internal staff from cross-club departments. - Develop and maintain an electronic monitoring and logging system for kit and equipment that provides live data of stock levels and planned distribution. - Ensuring the effective logistical planning for all required kit and equipment, attending home/away games as required in liaison with the Management and Coaching Staff. - Organise and co-ordinate all kit and equipment for away travel and the loading of club transport ready for departure. - Ensure all kit is fully laundered (washed and dried) correctly and packed away within the required timelines. - Maintain the quality and cleanliness of all kit and equipment assigned to LFCW. - Mark and maintain all kit with the relevant initials/numbers, as directed by the Operations Manager. - Maintain accurate records of all kit logs and report discrepancies to the Operations Manager.
What we are looking for:
The successful candidate will hold a clean driving licence and demonstrate meticulous attention to detail, consistently upholding high standards of behaviour and appearance. Previous experience in inventory management is essential, along with a background in working within a professional football club or sports-based environment, particularly with high-performance athletes. A clean driving licence and having the flexibility to travel nationally and internationally are essential. Candidates must possess certification in Safeguarding and Protecting Children (or FA equivalent) prior to starting the role. While not essential, the ability to speak additional languages and prior experience in the women’s game are highly desirable.
Please note, this vacancy may close early if we receive a high volume of applications, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.
To reward your hard work and commitment we offer a competitive salary, 25 days holiday (plus 8 bank holidays and the option to purchase up to an additional 5 days) and access to our benefits kit bag which includes but not limited to:
- Contributory pension scheme matching up to 5.5% - Life Assurance Cover - Free financial guidance and mortgage advice - Car salary sacrifice scheme for affordable driving - Cycle to Work scheme to keep you active - Purchase season ticket loans for easier commuting - Exclusive shopping discounts & cashback with top retailers - Will Writing Service for future planning - Employee Assistance Programme for confidential support - Medicash Health Cash Plan for everyday healthcare needs - Volunteering opportunities to give back to the community - Special LFC perks – retail discounts, partner offers, free LFCTV GO access, and priority tickets for matches, events & concerts
…and much more! LFC Benefits.pdf
At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.
Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment. This role is subject to a satisfactory enhanced DBS check.
#LFCJobs

job.title: Data Engineer
job.html_description:
We are recruiting for a Data Engineer to be responsible for the continued development and improvement of the club’s data platform. Responsibility for technical direction and architectural standards across data pipelines, warehouse infrastructure and model deployment.

Working closely with Research Systems, Data Science and Performance Insights, the Data Engineer ensures that our data infrastructure remains reliable, scalable and aligned with the evolving needs of the department.
job.plain_text_description:
We are recruiting for a Data Engineer to be responsible for the continued development and improvement of the club’s data platform. Responsibility for technical direction and architectural standards across data pipelines, warehouse infrastructure and model deployment.
Working closely with Research Systems, Data Science and Performance Insights, the Data Engineer ensures that our data infrastructure remains reliable, scalable and aligned with the evolving needs of the department.

job.title: Marketing Salesforce Administrator
job.html_description:
Are you a Salesforce Administrator with a passion for driving impactful marketing campaigns and transforming data into powerful insights? We’re looking for an energetic, forward‑thinking individual to join our Marketing team and play a key role in shaping the future of our club’s marketing and sales success.

In this role, you’ll take ownership of the management of our Salesforce environment, technically owning the development of the platform, sending out marketing campaigns and supporting the sales team to ensure that leads and contacts are managed efficiently.

As part of a highly collaborative team, you will support the club’s data and marketing technology capabilities, driving sales lead management, marketing automation, sales process optimisation, and insightful reporting. Your work will directly support our sales and account management teams to achieve ambitious targets in the acquisition and retention of Hospitality and non‑matchday events.

What will you be doing?

• Continue the enhancement of the platform by gathering requirements, investigating and proposing solutions, integrating with new apps and collaborating with third parties.

• Salesforce configuration and maintenance including Flow, fields, page layouts, record types and custom settings.

• Be the first point of contact for all daily system admin, user and access management, telephony management, support and training for all users.

• Collaborate with the wider Customer Marketing team with the planning of campaigns and be responsible for segmenting campaign audiences, testing, execution, and post campaign analysis of all Salesforce email campaigns.

• Use of key technologies, such as marketing automation, to drive efficiency in the sales process ensuring the most effective leads are acquired and prioritised for direct contact by the Sales Team.

• Produce a range of dashboards, reports and insight using existing club and third-party data enrichment to ensure senior management and the Sales Team have the necessary information to drive business decisions.

• Manage data quality within Salesforce, keep a monthly tracker of data quality changes and identify and rectify issues.

• Work with the Hospitality and Legal teams to ensure all products and electronic contracts are created and maintained within Salesforce available for the sales team to send to clients.

• Work with the Senior Manager – Marketing Data Environments to use data to help shape the Hospitality and Events sales and service strategies set by the Sales Director.

Who are we looking for?

To succeed in this role, you’ll bring a strong track record as a Salesforce Administrator and, ideally, hold a Salesforce Administrator certification. You will have hands-on experience delivering marketing campaigns in a B2B or B2C environment and be highly numerate, with advanced Microsoft Excel skills.

With a logical and analytical mindset, you’ll be confident working with large datasets, able to manipulate, segment, analyse and translate complex information into clear, actionable insights for stakeholders.

You will have excellent organisational and prioritisation skills, with the ability to work accurately under pressure and to tight deadlines. Strong interpersonal skills are essential, and you’ll thrive in a fast-paced marketing and sales environment. You’ll be equally comfortable working collaboratively as part of a team or independently.

Why should you apply?

This is a full-time perm position working 35 hours per week. Your main base will be our city centre office in Liverpool.

Please note, this vacancy may close early if we receive a high volume of applications, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

To reward your hard work and commitment we offer a competitive salary, 25 days holiday (plus 8 bank holidays and the option to purchase up to an additional 5 days) and access to our benefits kit bag which includes but not limited to:

· Contributory pension scheme matching up to 5.5%

· Life Assurance Cover

· Free financial guidance and mortgage advice

· Car salary sacrifice scheme for affordable driving

· Cycle to Work scheme to keep you active

· Purchase season ticket loans for easier commuting

· Exclusive shopping discounts & cashback with top retailers

· Will Writing Service for future planning

· Employee Assistance Programme for confidential support

· Medicash Health Cash Plan for everyday healthcare needs

· Volunteering opportunities to give back to the community

· Special LFC perks – retail discounts, partner offers, free LFCTV GO access, and priority tickets for matches, events & concerts … and much more! LFC Benefits.pdf

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football

Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

#LFCJobs
