job.title: Partnership Operations Manager
job.html_description:
We are looking for a Manager, Partnership Operations to join the team at Liverpool FC. 

As a Manager within the Partnerships Operations team, you will provide strategic direction and operational leadership across all partnership delivery activity. The role spans multiple LFC sites, including Anfield, St Helens Stadium, and the AXA Melwood Training Centre.

Operating at both a strategic and hands-on level, the post holder ensures the seamless execution of branding, activation, and event delivery across all venues. Acting as the central coordination point between the Partnership Delivery team and wider club stakeholders, the Partnership Operations Manager ensures that all partnership rights are delivered to the highest standard aligned with club values and reflective of a world‑class global sports organisation.

What will you be doing?

Multi- Site Operational Leadership 

  • Day to Day operational readiness across - Anfield, St Helens and AXA Melwood. 
  • Ensure all processes across sites are efficient and impactful. Lead on reviewing and updating processes as and when required.  
  • Ensure consistency in safety and presentation across all partner activations across all sites.  
  • Collaborate with internal departments and build strong relationships to ensure effective communication of partnership plans are filtered out across the club. 
  • Act as a senior escalation point for operational challenges, driving resolution and continuous improvement across sites. 

Partnership Branding & Delivery 

  • Align and work closely with the delivery team to ensure effective communication of contractual rights delivery of all rights – from planning to delivery stage. 
  • Provide strategic oversight of seasonal branding inventories (e.g. LED, matchday programme, static signage), ensuring optimisation and compliance 
  • Oversee the tracking of branding rights throughout the season e.g. matchday programme, LED etc. 
  • Overseeing the management and planning on the usage of rights – specifically Play on Pitch, Open Training, LED Boards, Static signage, Hospitality Branding, Dressing Room Branding, Fan zone activations, digital integrations and LFC Legends Match. 

Matchday, Fixture Operations & Events 

  • Ensure all relevant health and safety documentation is provided in a timely manner. 
  • Oversee contractor management, risk assessments, and method statements. 
  • Lead partnership operational contact for any adhoc matches held at Anfield eg Play On pitch. 
  • Oversee all partner related events, private hire or activity across all venues. 

 Team leadership 

  • Lead and develop the Partnerships Operations team, embedding clear processes, accountability and performance standards. 
  • Mentor the Senior Executive across operational delivery areas, providing strategic direction and professional development support 
  • Promote a positive and collaborative team culture 

Who are we looking for? 

The ideal candidate will hold a bachelor’s degree and bring proven experience in commercial partnerships, sponsorship operations, or similar roles within the sports or media industry. They will demonstrate strong project management capability, with the ability to prioritise effectively, manage timelines, and coordinate multiple stakeholders. Excellent leadership, organisational skills, and meticulous attention to detail are essential to ensure accuracy and consistency across operational processes and contract management. Strong communication and interpersonal skills are also key, enabling effective collaboration and relationship-building with both internal teams and external partners.

Why should you apply?

This is a full-time perm position working 35 hours per week. Your main base will be our city centre office in Liverpool.

Please note, this vacancy may close early if we receive a high volume of applications, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

To reward your hard work and commitment we offer a competitive salary, 25 days holiday (plus 8 bank holidays and the option to purchase up to an additional 5 days) and access to our benefits kit bag which includes but not limited to:

  • Contributory pension scheme matching up to 5.5%
  • Life Assurance Cover
  • Free financial guidance and mortgage advice
  • Car salary sacrifice scheme for affordable driving
  • Cycle to Work scheme to keep you active
  • Purchase season ticket loans for easier commuting
  • Exclusive shopping discounts & cashback with top retailers
  • Will Writing Service for future planning
  • Employee Assistance Programme for confidential support
  • Medicash Health Cash Plan for everyday healthcare needs
  • Volunteering opportunities to give back to the community
  • Special LFC perks – retail discounts, partner offers, free LFCTV GO access, and priority tickets for matches, events & concerts

…and much more! LFC Benefits.pdf

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

#LFCJobs

job.title: Casual Kitchen Assistant
job.html_description:
We are looking for experienced Kitchen Assistants to join our casual catering team at our AXA Training Centre. You’ll work in our state-of-the-art kitchens alongside our talented and creative chefs, supporting the delivery of first-class food and service.

 What will you be doing?

  • Assisting with basic food preparation.
  • Unloading and storing deliveries safely and efficiently.
  • Maintaining cleanliness by emptying bins, sweeping, and mopping floors.
  • Ensuring all kitchen equipment is cleaned and stored correctly.
  • Deep cleaning fridges, storage areas, and other kitchen spaces to maintain hygiene standards.
  • Operating automated dishwashers to clean crockery and utensils used by guests.
  • Supporting with porter duties, ensuring all back-of-house areas are tidy and ready for the next event.

Who are we looking for?

We’re looking for hardworking individuals who thrive in a fast-paced kitchen environment.

Please note you must be 18 or over to be considered for this position.

 You’ll have:

  • Previous experience as a Kitchen Assistant, Kitchen Porter, or in a similar role.
  • A good understanding of manual handling techniques and safe working practices.
  • Experience using a range of kitchen equipment.
  • A positive attitude and a commitment to maintaining high standards of cleanliness and teamwork.

Why should you apply?

This is a casual position, offering variable hours including evenings and weekends on a flexible assignment basis.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

job.title: Casual Match Day Line Chef
job.html_description:
We are currently recruiting for experienced Line Chefs to support our casual catering team.

This is a fast-paced role, ideal for chefs who are confident working in high-volume environments and delivering consistent, quality food service.

What will you be doing?

  • Preparing and cooking core match day menu items (e.g. burgers, chicken wings and other grab and go favourites).
  • Supporting with preparation and delivery of any match day specials (varies per fixture).
  • Ensuring food is prepared, cooked and presented to company standards.
  • Maintaining high standards of food hygiene, safety and cleanliness at all times.
  • Working collaboratively with the wider kitchen and hospitality teams to ensure smooth service.

Who are we looking for?

  • Previous experience at prep chef level or similar.
  • Level 2 Food Hygiene Certificate (essential).
  • Confident cooking a consistent menu in a high-volume environment.
  • Strong team player with a positive, reliable attitude.

Why should you apply?

This is a casual position, offering variable hours including evenings and weekends on a flexible assignment basis.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

job.title: In-House Employment Lawyer
job.html_description:
In-House Employment Lawyer

We have an exciting opportunity for an individual to join our Liverpool FC, in-house Legal team. We’re looking for an individual with expert commercial employment lawyer to provide pragmatic, timely advice across the Club, helping to ensure our people practices, decision making and policies comply with employment legislation, data protection requirements and international employment standards.

In this high volume, fast paced environment, you’ll report to the Vice President, Legal and partner closely with the People & Culture team, offering hands on case management., and proactive legal insight to minimise risk and strengthen internal capability. 

You will be expected to handle complex ER casework, DSARs, contractual queries and global employment issues. 

What will you be doing?

Employment Law Advise 

  • Provide expert, commercially focused employment law advice to leaders and People & Culture teams and business leaders.. 
  • Advise across all areas of UK employment law, 
  • Monitor developments in employment legislation and ensure the Club remains compliant and future‑ready. 
  • Produce clear legal guidance, risk assessments and recommended options. 

Complex Case Management 

  • Lead ER cases 
  • Guide the ER Manager and People team 
  • Coach managers and People Partners on case strategy and risk management.  

Data Protection & DSARs 

  • Lead all employment‑related DSAR responses in partnership with People Services and Data Protection teams.
  • Advise on GDPR compliance in investigations, suspensions and information sharing.
  • Recommend process improvements to reduce risk and improve efficiency. 

Contracts, Policies & Governance 

  • Draft and review employment contracts, senior agreements, and people policies.
  • Ensure policies are legally compliant and aligned with Club culture.
  • Support global/overseas contract implementation and compliance. 

Global & Overseas Employment Support 

  • Provide employment law advice for international hires, assignments and cross‑border working. 
  • Ensure alignment with local labour laws and global mobility requirements. 

Stakeholder Management & Collaboration

  • Build strong partnerships across Legal, People & Culture, Safeguarding, Finance and operational teams.
  • Manage relationships with external legal advisers to ensure quality and cost control.

Capability Building & Continuous Improvement 

  • Deliver training on employment law, ER processes, DSARs and governance. 
  • Embed strong legal reasoning across people processes to reduce risk. 
  • Promote consistent, transparent and fair decision‑making across the Club

Who are we looking for?

  • Able to translate complex legal principles into clear, practical, and commercially focused advice.
  • Handle sensitive information with discretion, sound judgment, and absolute integrity.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
  • Confident presenting to senior stakeholders.
  • Strong analytical ability and able to work accurately under pressure.
  • Highly organised, with the ability to manage a high volume of varied work.
  • Collaborative, people‑focused and solutions‑oriented approach.
  • Acts as a role model for the Club’s values, demonstrating professionalism, trust and accountability in all situations.
  • Calm, pragmatic, and with strong attention detail.
  • Committed to continuous improvement and developing capability across the organisation.
  • Qualified solicitor (England & Wales) with minimum 5 years’ PQE in employment law.
  • Current practicing certificate (SRA) with up-to-date CPD 
  • Strong background in high risk, complex employee relations and Employment Tribunal litigation.
  • Experience working in fast paced, high profile or highly regulated environments (e.g. sport, media, large corporates).
  • Demonstrable experience handling DSARs, GDPR related issues and data heavy case management.
  • Confident in drafting and negotiating employment contracts, policies, settlement agreements and international terms.

Desirable Experience

  • Experience advising on overseas employment matters or global mobility 
  • Experience of working in house 
  • Experience of commercial legal work 

Please note, this vacancy may close early if we receive a high volume of applications, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

To reward your hard work and commitment we offer a competitive salary, 25 days holiday (plus 8 bank holidays and the option to purchase up to an additional 5 days) and access to our benefits kit bag which includes but not limited to:

  • Contributory pension scheme matching up to 5.5%
  • Life Assurance Cover
  • Free financial guidance and mortgage advice
  • Car salary sacrifice scheme for affordable driving
  • Cycle to Work scheme to keep you active
  • Purchase season ticket loans for easier commuting
  • Exclusive shopping discounts & cashback with top retailers
  • Will Writing Service for future planning
  • Employee Assistance Programme for confidential support
  • Medicash Health Cash Plan for everyday healthcare needs
  • Volunteering opportunities to give back to the community
  • Special LFC perks – retail discounts, partner offers, free LFCTV GO access, and priority tickets for matches, events & concerts

…and much more! LFC Benefits.pdf

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

#LFCJobs
job.plain_text_description:
In-House Employment Lawyer
We have an exciting opportunity for an individual to join our Liverpool FC, in-house Legal team. We’re looking for an individual with expert commercial employment lawyer to provide pragmatic, timely advice across the Club, helping to ensure our people practices, decision making and policies comply with employment legislation, data protection requirements and international employment standards.
In this high volume, fast paced environment, you’ll report to the Vice President, Legal and partner closely with the People & Culture team, offering hands on case management., and proactive legal insight to minimise risk and strengthen internal capability.
You will be expected to handle complex ER casework, DSARs, contractual queries and global employment issues.
What will you be doing?
Employment Law Advise
- Provide expert, commercially focused employment law advice to leaders and People & Culture teams and business leaders.. - Advise across all areas of UK employment law, - Monitor developments in employment legislation and ensure the Club remains compliant and future‑ready. - Produce clear legal guidance, risk assessments and recommended options.
Complex Case Management
- Lead ER cases - Guide the ER Manager and People team - Coach managers and People Partners on case strategy and risk management.
Data Protection & DSARs
- Lead all employment‑related DSAR responses in partnership with People Services and Data Protection teams. - Advise on GDPR compliance in investigations, suspensions and information sharing. - Recommend process improvements to reduce risk and improve efficiency.
Contracts, Policies & Governance
- Draft and review employment contracts, senior agreements, and people policies. - Ensure policies are legally compliant and aligned with Club culture. - Support global/overseas contract implementation and compliance.
Global & Overseas Employment Support
- Provide employment law advice for international hires, assignments and cross‑border working. - Ensure alignment with local labour laws and global mobility requirements.
Stakeholder Management & Collaboration
- Build strong partnerships across Legal, People & Culture, Safeguarding, Finance and operational teams. - Manage relationships with external legal advisers to ensure quality and cost control.
Capability Building & Continuous Improvement
- Deliver training on employment law, ER processes, DSARs and governance. - Embed strong legal reasoning across people processes to reduce risk. - Promote consistent, transparent and fair decision‑making across the Club
Who are we looking for?
- Able to translate complex legal principles into clear, practical, and commercially focused advice. - Handle sensitive information with discretion, sound judgment, and absolute integrity. - Excellent written and verbal communication skills. - Confident presenting to senior stakeholders. - Strong analytical ability and able to work accurately under pressure. - Highly organised, with the ability to manage a high volume of varied work. - Collaborative, people‑focused and solutions‑oriented approach. - Acts as a role model for the Club’s values, demonstrating professionalism, trust and accountability in all situations. - Calm, pragmatic, and with strong attention detail. - Committed to continuous improvement and developing capability across the organisation. - Qualified solicitor (England & Wales) with minimum 5 years’ PQE in employment law. - Current practicing certificate (SRA) with up-to-date CPD - Strong background in high risk, complex employee relations and Employment Tribunal litigation. - Experience working in fast paced, high profile or highly regulated environments (e.g. sport, media, large corporates). - Demonstrable experience handling DSARs, GDPR related issues and data heavy case management. - Confident in drafting and negotiating employment contracts, policies, settlement agreements and international terms.
Desirable Experience
- Experience advising on overseas employment matters or global mobility - Experience of working in house - Experience of commercial legal work
Please note, this vacancy may close early if we receive a high volume of applications, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.
To reward your hard work and commitment we offer a competitive salary, 25 days holiday (plus 8 bank holidays and the option to purchase up to an additional 5 days) and access to our benefits kit bag which includes but not limited to:
- Contributory pension scheme matching up to 5.5% - Life Assurance Cover - Free financial guidance and mortgage advice - Car salary sacrifice scheme for affordable driving - Cycle to Work scheme to keep you active - Purchase season ticket loans for easier commuting - Exclusive shopping discounts & cashback with top retailers - Will Writing Service for future planning - Employee Assistance Programme for confidential support - Medicash Health Cash Plan for everyday healthcare needs - Volunteering opportunities to give back to the community - Special LFC perks – retail discounts, partner offers, free LFCTV GO access, and priority tickets for matches, events & concerts
…and much more! LFC Benefits.pdf

We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all.
